questions.question: The Dravida style of Temple architecture came into vogue during which of the following period?
questions.explanation: During the Chola administration, the temple architecture in the south reached its climax. The Dravida style of Temple architecture came into vogue which laid emphasis on the building of storey upon storey above the chief deity room.

questions.question: The Sangam literature is most likely to have been composed during which of the following centuries?
questions.explanation: The style and certain historical references in the poems of the Sangam period suggests that this literature is most likely to have been composed between 3rd Century BCE and 3rd century CE.

questions.question: Which UNESCO World Heritage site has Temple of Virupaksha as its part?
questions.explanation: Group of Monuments at Pattadakal comprises of Temple of Virupaksha, built by Queen Lokamahadevi in c.740 to commemorate the victory of her husband.

questions.question: Which of these is a knwon Buddhist Literature?
questions.explanation: Tripitaka is a traditional term used by various Buddhist sects to describe their various canons of scriptures. As the name suggests, a Tripitaka traditionally contains three “baskets” of teachings: a Sutra Pitaka, a Vinaya Pitaka and an Abhidharma Pitaka. Tripitaka is the three main categories of texts that make up the Buddhist canon. It is the oldest collection Buddhist scriptures.

questions.question: At which location a sculptor of chariot from Harappa civilization was found?
questions.explanation: Daimabad in Ahmednagar district of Maharashtrahas yield a sculpture of a chariot belonging to the Harappan time. The chariot is 45 cm long and 16 cm wide, yoked to two oxen, driven by a man 16 cm high standing in it. The site is famous for the recovery of many bronze goods influenced by the Harappan culture.

questions.question: In which year did Kalinga war take place?
questions.explanation: The Kalinga war was fought between the Maurya Empire under Ashoka the Great and the state of Kalinga, a feudal republic located on the coast of the present-day Indian state of Odisha and nothern parts of Andhra Pradesh. On the basis of Ashokan inscriptions, it can be established that it was fought in 262- 261 BC. The Kalinga war, the only major war Ashoka fought after his accession to throne, is one of the major and bloodiest battles in the history of India.

questions.question: Which among the given Gupta kings stopped the Huns from invading India__?
questions.explanation: Skandagupta was a Gupta Emperor of northern India. He crushed the Hun invasion in 455, & managed to keep them at bay; however, the expense of the wars drained the empire’s resources & contributed to its decline. He is generally considered the last of the great Gupta Emperors who faced some of the greatest challenges in the annals of the empire having to contend with the Pushyamitras & the Hunas (a name by which the “White Huns” were known in India).

questions.question: Who is the real founder of Jainism?
questions.explanation: Lord Rishabhdev is the first Tirthankara of Jainism. Tirthankara means one who creates a pilgrimage. Those who create a pilgrimage from the world ocean (the cycle of birth and death) to salvation are called Tirthankaras. Rishabhdev Ji is also called Adinath. Lord Rishabhdev is the first Tirthankara of the present Avarspini period.

questions.question: Consider the following statements and choose the correct answer from the options given below: Assertion (A): Harappans were prolific long distance traders. Reason (R): Harappans most likely practiced barter exchange as we have not found any evidence of metallic money.

options.label: A
options.text: Both, A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.

options.label: B
options.text: Both, A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

options.label: C
options.text: A is true and R is false.

options.label: D
options.text: A is false and R is true.

answer.label: B
answer.text: Both, A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.explanation: One of the defining features of the urbanisation is the ‘long distance trade’. Many of the Harappan cities were exclusively settled to make various artefacts that were traded over a long distance e.g. Chanhudaro. But, these Harappan cities often did not possess necessary raw materials for the commodities they produced. So, trade was going on in both raw materials as well as finished goods. Harappans had trading relations with Rajasthan, Afghanistan, Persia, Mesopotamia, Bahrain and Central Asia, etc. However, we have not found any evidence of metal currency. Seals and sealings give testament of trade but they cannot work as currency. Most probably, Harappans traded finished and food grains in return for raw material. Currency normally facilitates long distance trade but its absence did not seem to be a particular problem for Harappans. So, both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.question: Consider the following statements and choose the correct answer from the options given below: Assertion (A): Contact with the Mesopotamian cities may have provided some stimulus to the development of the Harappan culture. Reason (R): Town planning of both these cultures was remarkably similar.

options.label: A
options.text: Both, A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.

options.label: B
options.text: Both, A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

options.label: C
options.text: A is true and R is false.

options.label: D
options.text: A is false and R is true.

answer.label: C
answer.text: A is true and R is false.

questions.explanation: Long distance trade played an important role in the rise Harappan Civilisation and the trade with Mesopotamia was a major part of it. Also, both these cultures adopted some cultural features from each other. Seals of both these cultures have been found from the sites of other culture. So, A is true. But, this stimulus should not be confused as the genesis of Harappan civilisation. Certain elements distinguish the Harappan culture from Mesopotamia. One such element is the town planning. While the Harappan cities were well planned, the Mesopotamian cities show a haphazard growth. So, R is false.
